\begin{document}

\title{Domed Monuments Cluster at Babylonian B\={e}ru Harmonics:\\
A Longitude Enrichment Test on the UNESCO Cultural and Mixed Heritage Corpus}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Fourth Temple Research, USA}

\maketitle

\abstract[ABSTRACT]{
Domed monuments in the UNESCO World Heritage Cultural and Mixed corpus
cluster at harmonics of the Babylonian b\={e}ru (30\textdegree{},
MUL.APIN, c.~1000~BCE) when longitudes are referenced to Mount Gerizim,
a historically attested meridian anchor.
The full corpus ($N=\NclusterTotal$) shows significant enrichment within
one distance-b\={e}ru (${\lesssim}\ApThreshKm{}$~km) of a harmonic
(Bonferroni $p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestOne$~\pAdjTestOneSig{}, $k=\BonfK$).
Among dome/spherical sites ($N=\NcircTotal$), this enrichment is
$\circEnrichAp\times$ the null (Fisher OR~$=\circApFisherOR$ relative to
full-corpus baseline, Bonferroni $p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestTwo$~\pAdjTestTwoSig{});
sites within one parasang (${\lesssim}\AppThreshKm{}$~km, the finer sub-unit)
show $\circEnrichApp\times$ enrichment, surviving full Bonferroni at $k=\NtotalTests$.
Cluster asymmetry independently reaches significance
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestThree$~\pAdjTestThreeSig{}).
The anchor is historically motivated; the pipeline is fully reproducible.
}

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
longitude periodicity, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
Babylonian b\={e}ru, ancient metrology, domed architecture, archaeoastronomy,
monument distribution, MUL.APIN

\bigskip
